response:
1. The width of the golden rectangle is given as 2 meters. The golden ratio φ is defined as φ = (1 + √5) / 2 ≈ 1.618. The length of the rectangle is calculated using the golden ratio:

$$
\text{Length} = \phi \times \text{Width} = \phi \times 2 = 2 \times \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} = 1 + \sqrt{5}
$$

The area of the rectangle is:

$$
\text{Area} = \text{Length} \times \text{Width} = (1 + \sqrt{5}) \times 2 = 2 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$

The diagonal of the rectangle can be found using the Pythagorean theorem:

$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{\text{Length}^2 + \text{Width}^2} = \sqrt{(1 + \sqrt{5})^2 + 2^2}
$$

Expanding the square:

$$
(1 + \sqrt{5})^2 = 1 + 2\sqrt{5} + 5 = 6 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$

$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{6 + 2\sqrt{5} + 4} = \sqrt{10 + 2\sqrt{5}}
$$

2. The circle inscribed within the golden rectangle touches all four sides of the rectangle. The diameter of the circle is equal to the width of the rectangle, which is 2 meters. Therefore, the radius of the circle is:

$$
\text{Radius} = \frac{\text{Diameter}}{2} = \frac{2}{2} = 1 \text{ meter}
$$

The area of the region inside the rectangle but outside the circle is the area of the rectangle minus the area of the circle:

$$
\text{Area of rectangle outside circle} = (2 + 2\sqrt{5}) - \pi \times 1^2 = 2 + 2\sqrt{5} - \pi
$$

This is the exact area of the region inside the rectangle but outside the circle.
